name: Black & Whites
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, cream together the sugar and white shortening until light and fluffy.
2. Stir in the dry milk solids and corn syrup.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. In a separate bowl, sift together the cake flour, baking powder, and salt.
5. Alternately add the dry ingredients to the creamed mixture with water, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Stir in vanilla.
6. Scoop out 1/3 cup of batter and place it on a greased and floured baking sheet.
7. Smooth into a 3 1/2-inch circle. The batter will spread considerably, so do not bake more than four cookies per sheet at a time.
8. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 18 minutes or until lightly browned.
9. Cool on a rack.
10. Frost half of the cookies with chocolate fondant and half with vanilla frosting. Hold one cookie over the bowl while frosting. Work quickly as the frosting is warm.
FONDANT ICING
- Water (3/4 cup)
- Confectioner's sugar (2 cups)
- Light corn syrup (1/4 cup)
- Vanilla extract (1/2 teaspoon)
- Sweet chocolate (2 ounces)
In a large saucepan, combine water, confectioner's sugar, corn syrup, and vanilla. Cook over medium heat until the mixture reaches 100 degrees. For chocolate fondant, melt 2 ounces of sweet chocolate and mix with 1 1/2 cups of the batter.
